reasoning: The baseline option-adjusted spread (OAS) for CCC-rated bonds is approximately 9.14% as of June 20, 2025. This value is notably compressed compared to the long-term historical 25-year average of 11.33%. With spreads already experiencing recent volatility (spiking to 10.76% in April 2025), current levels represent relative tightness. Looking forward, there is substantial evidence pointing toward spread widening in H2 2025: macroeconomic forecasts by Barclays expect spread weakness due to lower carry, and Q3 earnings estimates predict potential drops that historically correlate with widening high-yield spreads. Furthermore, the FRED statistical trend model for this specific index gives a 70% chance of a short-term increase by late June based on recent upward momentum (9.04 to 9.14 in mid-June) and seasonal patterns. Therefore, I anticipate the spread will likely be higher than the June 22 baseline across the target dates. In accordance with the instruction to increase uncertainty over longer horizons, my probabilities taper steadily toward 0.5 for the more distant resolution dates.
